fresh_facts.claim: US maintains a naval blockade on Iranian ships and ports in the Strait of Hormuz, imposed after Iran restricted traffic due to Lebanon fighting.

President Trump stated on April 18 the US blockade 'will remain in full force' until a peace deal including Iran's nuclear program is reached, and warned of resuming strikes if no agreement by April 22.
Prior US warships USS Michael Murphy and USS Frank E. Peterson faced IRGC missile locks last week during a standoff, but no shots were fired.
Iran's parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf threatened to close the Strait if US pressure continues.
fresh_facts.source: Iranian government statement via social media

fresh_facts.claim: Iran announced on April 18 a 'new maritime order' requiring commercial vessels to obtain IRGC Navy authorization, pay tolls, and use approved routes near Iranian waters.
IRGC Navy warned and targeted US warships with 16 cruise missiles last week, forcing a high-risk US maneuver.
A US-Iran ceasefire pausing 7 weeks of Israel-US-Iran war is set to end April 22, with second US-Iran talks scheduled for April 20 in Islamabad.
US-Iran ceasefire talks initiated in Islamabad on April 11 collapsed due to sharp disagreements within Iran's negotiating team, leading to their return to Tehran.
A US military blockade of Iranian ports and coastal areas is set to begin April 20, driving oil prices above $100 per barrel.
UK PM Sir Keir Starmer stated on April 13 that the UK will not join the war or support the blockade of Iran.
Iran's state media criticized Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi for announcing Hormuz reopening, accusing him of creating confusion and aiding US claims of victory.
President Masoud Pezeshkian faces fierce hardline backlash for signaling conditional war-end willingness, exposing rifts with military leaders including IRGC chief Ahmad Vahidi.
IRGC chief Ahmad Vahidi and other hardliners pressured President Pezeshkian's appointment of Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr to the Supreme National Security Council.

Tensions between President Pezeshkian and IRGC chief Vahidi center on war management and economic fallout, pushing the president into 'complete political deadlock' with IRGC assuming key state functions.
Iranian hardliners branded ex-Foreign Minister Mohammad Javad Zarif a 'traitor' for war-end calls and urged arrests of him and ex-President Hassan Rouhani.
Iran's central bank warned President Pezeshkian that rebuilding the war-damaged economy could take over a decade.
Iran's economy shows structural crisis with high inflation, currency distrust, and a shifting monetary regime.
US nuclear demands shifted from permanent ban to a 20-year freeze, while Iran seeks a 5-year freeze.
Pakistan is mediating the ceasefire between US and Iran and pushing for new talks.
Israel's strikes hit Iranian repression infrastructure including intelligence, police, and Basij facilities, potentially weakening control.
Iran proposed Hormuz reopening tied to Lebanon ceasefire as a negotiation condition.

regime_fall.current_status: Iran escalates brinkmanship with a 'new maritime order' and Hormuz closure threats while facing a hardening US blockade deadline (April 20-22), internal power struggles between President Pezeshkian and IRGC chief Vahidi over war management, and economic warnings of decade-long reconstruction. Ceasefire talks collapsed amid negotiating team rifts, with second round scheduled for April 20 in Islamabad before the April 22 deadline for resuming US strikes.
regime_fall.last_major_event: President Trump declared on April 18 that the US blockade 'will remain in full force' until a nuclear peace deal is reached, setting an April 22 deadline for resumed military strikes while Iran announced a new maritime toll system and threatened Strait of Hormuz closure.
